source_label: Red Hat — What are SPIFFE and SPIRE? (SPIFFE, pronounced "spiffy")
notes: SPIFFE (Secure Production Identity Framework For Everyone) is a CNCF graduated standard that gives every workload a cryptographic identity (an SVID) so services can do zero-trust mTLS without baked-in secrets; its implementation is SPIRE. It is spoken as a word, not letter-by-letter, and the trailing -E is voiced: it is pronounced exactly like the English word "spiffy", rhyming with jiffy. The two traps are saying "spiff" with a silent E, or spelling it out S-P-I-F-F-E.
